prefecthq/prefect#19250.body:
### Bug summary

Example flow:
```
from prefect import flow, task
from prefect.concurrency.sync import concurrency
from prefect.futures import wait


@task(tags=["some_tag"])
def process_data(x, y):
    return x + y


@flow
def my_flow():
    futures = []
    for x, y in [(1, 2), (2, 3), (3, 4), (4, 5)]:
        futures.append(process_data.submit(x, y))

    wait(futures)


if __name__ == "__main__":
    my_flow()
```
Since the new v2 concurrency limits were introduced, about half the logs for a flow like this (which does not directly use concurrency limits in any way) are warnings about concurrency limits:
```
16:08:30.257 | INFO    | Flow run 'resilient-lion' - View at https://app.prefect.cloud/account/1e4d7e04-0fb7-4aa3-8ef5-746e9f404f4f/workspace/849a6829-4afb-48c3-9cc2-2dc6b262fd9c/runs/flow-run/068f7e83-da88-7ec1-8000-f121d3cd6eb8
16:08:30.523 | WARNING | Task run 'process_data-e3c' - Concurrency limits ['tag:some_tag'] do not exist - skipping acquisition.
16:08:30.528 | WARNING | Task run 'process_data-d6c' - Concurrency limits ['tag:some_tag'] do not exist - skipping acquisition.
16:08:30.564 | WARNING | Task run 'process_data-089' - Concurrency limits ['tag:some_tag'] do not exist - skipping acquisition.
16:08:30.565 | WARNING | Task run 'process_data-23b' - Concurrency limits ['tag:some_tag'] do not exist - skipping acquisition.
16:08:30.712 | INFO    | Task run 'process_data-e3c' - Finished in state Completed()
16:08:30.734 | INFO    | Task run 'process_data-089' - Finished in state Completed()
16:08:30.735 | INFO    | Task run 'process_data-d6c' - Finished in state Completed()
16:08:30.739 | INFO    | Task run 'process_data-23b' - Finished in state Completed()
16:08:30.781 | WARNING | Task run 'process_data-089' - Concurrency lease renewal failed - slots are no longer reserved. Execution will continue, but concurrency limits may be exceeded.
16:08:30.925 | INFO    | Flow run 'resilient-lion' - Finished in state Completed()
```
To me none of these should be WARNING level or even INFO for that matter: their importance is much lower than the standard flow/task run completion messages which are just INFO.

It's not totally straightforward to filter these out, can they perhaps be removed unless the limit was explicitly requested by the user (or maybe not even then...)?

prefecthq/prefect#19253.body:
### Bug summary

PrefectDbtRunner doesn't correctly handle parameters for dbt cli commands that have multiple words.

A check [here](https://github.com/PrefectHQ/prefect/blob/main/src/integrations/prefect-dbt/prefect_dbt/core/runner.py#L665-L666) will omit possibilities to pass e.g., `--profiles-dir` and `--project-dir` since these are not valid on command `dbt source` but on `dbt source freshness`. This results in the PrefectDbtSettings object not being respected for this case.

[I found an old similar issue for DbtCoreOperation](https://github.com/PrefectHQ/prefect/issues/13612).

This is stopping us from upgrading from the deprecated DbtCoreOperation to PrefectDbtRunner. I've tried to explicitly pass these configs to the invoke command(s) but can't get that to work either.

prefecthq/prefect#19256.body:
### Bug summary

**Problem**: When you have a work pool (cloud run push pool in our case) with env vars set, these apply globally to all deployments deployed to that work pool. However, if any deployments have their own env vars set as job variables, all of the work pool env vars are ignored. 

**Expected Behavior**: The expected behavior would be that these env vars would be merge with the deployment env vars overriding any work pool env vars. 

This is effectively the same as [issue](https://github.com/PrefectHQ/prefect/issues/11041) that was closed with the following [PR](https://github.com/PrefectHQ/prefect/pull/14932), however, the PR appears to have only handled the case for merging flow run env vars with deployment env vars but does not address merging deployment env vars with work pool env vars. The conclusion was that for env vars, a deep merge made sense and we would expect this to apply for deployment and work pool env vars. The current behavior expects that any deployment that declares env vars to be aware of all work pool env vars.


## Minimal Example
1. Work pool with env vars set
2. Deployment with a different set of env vars set
3. All flow runs of the deployment with env vars set will only have the env vars from the deployment and will not have any of the env vars from the work pool 

`flow.py`
```
@flow(name="env-vars-flow", log_prints=True)
def env_vars_flow():
    """Prints all environment variable keys."""
    print(sorted(os.environ.keys()))
```

`prefect.yaml`
```
name: prefect-sandbox
prefect-version: 3.4.24

build: null

push: null

pull:
  - prefect.deployments.steps.git_clone:
      ...

deployments:
  - name: env-vars-without
    entrypoint: prefect_sandbox/flows.py:env_vars_flow
    work_pool: 
      name: cloud-run-push-pool
      job_variables:
        image: ...

  - name: env-vars-with
    entrypoint: prefect_sandbox/flows.py:env_vars_flow
    work_pool:
      name: cloud-run-push-pool
      job_variables:
        image: ...
        env:
          DEPLOYMENT_ENV_VAR: "1"
```

Here we have two deployments of the same flow which simply logs the available env vars. when running the `env-vars-without` deployment we get the following log: 
```
['CLOUD_RUN_EXECUTION', 'CLOUD_RUN_JOB', 'CLOUD_RUN_TASK_ATTEMPT', 'CLOUD_RUN_TASK_COUNT', 'CLOUD_RUN_TASK_INDEX', 'GPG_KEY', 'HOME', 'LANG', 'LOGFIRE_TOKEN', 'PATH', 'PREFECT_API_KEY', 'PREFECT_API_URL', 'PREFECT_LOGGING_EXTRA_LOGGERS', 'PREFECT_LOGGING_LEVEL', 'PREFECT__FLOW_RUN_ID', 'PWD', 'PYTHON_SHA256', 'PYTHON_VERSION', 'USE_ADCS', 'UV_TOOL_BIN_DIR']
```
and when running the `env-vars-with` deployment we get the following log:
```
['CLOUD_RUN_EXECUTION', 'CLOUD_RUN_JOB', 'CLOUD_RUN_TASK_ATTEMPT', 'CLOUD_RUN_TASK_COUNT', 'CLOUD_RUN_TASK_INDEX', 'DEPLOYMENT_ENV_VAR', 'GPG_KEY', 'HOME', 'LANG', 'PATH', 'PREFECT_API_KEY', 'PREFECT_API_URL', 'PREFECT__FLOW_RUN_ID', 'PWD', 'PYTHON_SHA256', 'PYTHON_VERSION', 'USE_ADCS', 'UV_TOOL_BIN_DIR']
```

Of note, the env vars we have configured for our work pool only show up when running the `env-vars-without` deployment which didn't have deployment env vars. These were the `LOGFIRE_TOKEN`,  `PREFECT_LOGGING_LEVEL`, `PREFECT_LOGGING_EXTRA_LOGGERS`. In the `env-vars-with` deployment, none of those are present and we only see the `DEPLOYMENT_ENV_VAR` that was explicitly specified in the deployment.

### Version info

```Text
Version:              3.4.24
API version:          0.8.4
Python version:       3.13.3
Git commit:           0472bb5a
Built:                Thu, Oct 16, 2025 06:36 PM
OS/Arch:              darwin/arm64
Profile:              dr
Server type:          cloud
Pydantic version:     2.12.3
Server:
  Database:           sqlite
  SQLite version:     3.47.1

I have seen this issue on 3.2.x as well.
```

_No response_

prefecthq/prefect#19257.body:
### Bug summary

**Problem**: As of ~ 2 weeks ago, we are no longer able to pass `datetime.date` and `datetime.datetime` values as parameters to `run_deployment`. Previously, this worked as expected kicking off a flow run with the appropriate parameters, however, we now get the following error: `prefect.exceptions.PrefectHTTPStatusError: Client error '409 Conflict' for url ... Response: {'detail': "Error creating flow run: Validation failed for field 'dt'. Failure reason: 1761142709.295396 is not of type 'string'"}`

This may be related to the following [issue](https://github.com/PrefectHQ/prefect/issues/18907)

There is a relatively simple workaround of casting values to `str` when passing them in, however, this is definitely not expected behavior, especially considering that this used to work as expected.

## Minimal Example
1. Create flow with a `datetime.date` or `datetime.datetime` parameter
2. Create a flow run of that flow using `run_deployment` passing in a `datetime.date` or `datetime.datetime` value

`flow.py`
```
import datetime

from prefect import flow, get_run_logger, task
from prefect.deployments import run_deployment
from prefect.futures import PrefectFutureList
from prefect.server.schemas.states import StateType


@task(retries=0)
def run_deployment_task(deployment: str, parameters: dict):
    """A task for running a deployment."""
    flow_run_result = run_deployment(
        deployment,
        parameters=parameters,
    )

    if flow_run_result.state_type in [
        StateType.FAILED,
        StateType.CRASHED,
        StateType.CANCELLED,
        StateType.CANCELLING,
    ]:
        raise Exception(f"Failed with status {flow_run_result.state_type}")


@flow(name="base-date-flow")
def base_date_flow(date: datetime.date):
    """A flow to testing prefect deployment changes."""
    logger = get_run_logger()
    logger.debug(date)


@flow(name="base-datetime-flow")
def base_datetime_flow(dt: datetime.datetime):
    """A flow to testing prefect deployment changes."""
    logger = get_run_logger()
    logger.debug(dt)


@flow(name="orchestrating-flow")
def orchestrating_flow():
    """A flow to testing prefect deployment changes."""
    task_futures = PrefectFutureList()

    task_futures.append(
        run_deployment_task.submit(
            deployment="base-datetime-flow/base-datetime-deployment",
            parameters={"dt": str(datetime.datetime.now())},
        )
    )
    task_futures.append(
        run_deployment_task.submit(
            deployment="base-date-flow/base-date-deployment",
            parameters={"date": str(datetime.date.today())},
        )
    )
    task_futures.append(
        run_deployment_task.submit(
            deployment="base-datetime-flow/base-datetime-deployment",
            parameters={"dt": datetime.datetime.now()},
        )
    )
    task_futures.append(
        run_deployment_task.submit(
            deployment="base-date-flow/base-date-deployment",
            parameters={"date": datetime.date.today()},
        )
    )

    task_futures.result()

```

`prefect.yaml`
```
name: prefect-sandbox
prefect-version: 3.4.24

build: null

push: null

pull:
  - prefect.deployments.steps.git_clone:
      ...

deployments:
  - name: orchestrating-deployment
    tags: ["testing", "sandbox"]
    description: A dummy flow for testing prefect deployments
    entrypoint: prefect_sandbox/flows.py:orchestrating_flow
    work_pool: *cloud-run-pool

  - name: base-date-deployment
    tags: ["testing", "sandbox"]
    description: A dummy flow for testing prefect deployments
    entrypoint: prefect_sandbox/flows.py:base_date_flow
    work_pool: *cloud-run-pool

  - name: base-datetime-deployment
    tags: ["testing", "sandbox"]
    description: A dummy flow for testing prefect deployments
    entrypoint: prefect_sandbox/flows.py:base_datetime_flow
    work_pool: *cloud-run-pool
```

When we run the `orchestrating-deployment` it triggers flow runs for each of the base deployments twice, once with the values as their expected types, and once with the values cast as strings. The flow runs that get `str` values passed in run as expected. The other flow runs are never even created due to `run_deployment` failing with the following errors
```
prefect.exceptions.PrefectHTTPStatusError: Client error '409 Conflict' for url ... Response: {'detail': "Error creating flow run: Validation failed for field 'dt'. Failure reason: 1761142709.295396 is not of type 'string'"}
```
Clearly the value passed in is not `1761142709.295396` rather a `datetime.datetime` object in this case and it appears that serialization for these values was inadvertently changed and they are no longer handled as expected.

We have experienced this issue across versions 3.2.X and 3.4.X starting roughly two weeks ago. Prior to that we have been doing this successfully for at least 6 months.

### Version info

```Text
Version:              3.4.24
API version:          0.8.4
Python version:       3.13.3
Git commit:           0472bb5a
Built:                Thu, Oct 16, 2025 06:36 PM
OS/Arch:              darwin/arm64
Profile:              dr
Server type:          cloud
Pydantic version:     2.12.3
Server:
  Database:           sqlite
  SQLite version:     3.47.1

I have seen this issue on 3.2.x as well.
```

### Additional context

_No response_
prefecthq/prefect#19257.title: Improper handling of `datetime` parameters in `run_deployment`

prefecthq/prefect#19276.body:
## current behavior

flows support an `on_running` hook that allows users to execute custom functions when a flow starts running.

tasks currently only support `on_completion` and `on_failure` hooks but are missing `on_running`.

## desired behavior

tasks should support an `on_running` hook to allow users to execute initialization logic when a task starts running.

## use case

users want to call a list of functions at the start of each task without having to manually add boilerplate to every task function. this is particularly useful for:
- common logging/monitoring setup
- resource initialization
- metric collection

## workaround

currently, users must manually add hook calls at the beginning of each task:

```python
@task
def some_task():
    _run_fns(partial(fn1, **kw1), ...)
    
    # task logic
```

having a proper `on_running` hook would eliminate this repetitive pattern.

## implementation notes

- tasks are orchestrated client-side for performance, while flows are orchestrated server-side
- need to ensure the hook is called at the appropriate point in the client-side task orchestration lifecycle
- should maintain consistency with flow hook behavior where possible
prefecthq/prefect#19276.title: Add on_running hook for tasks

prefecthq/prefect#19288.body:
### Bug summary

After #19119 was merged we're seeing some issues with `Flow.validate_parameters()`

The problem is that we're sending along pydantic models as part of the parameter and as such run into issues such as:

```python
2025-10-27T12:09:49.972149Z [error    ] Unhandled exception occurred in resolvers [infrahub]
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/Users/patrick/.virtualenvs/infrahub/lib/python3.12/site-packages/graphql/execution/execute.py", line 530, in await_result
    return_type, field_nodes, info, path, await result
                                          ^^^^^^^^^^^^
  File "/Users/patrick/.virtualenvs/infrahub/lib/python3.12/site-packages/opentelemetry/util/_decorator.py", line 69, in async_wrapper
    return await func(*args, **kwargs)  # type: ignore
           ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
  File "/Users/patrick/Code/opsmill/infrahub/backend/infrahub/graphql/mutations/branch.py", line 93, in mutate
    await graphql_context.active_service.workflow.execute_workflow(
  File "/Users/patrick/Code/opsmill/infrahub/backend/infrahub/services/adapters/workflow/local.py", line 30, in execute_workflow
    parameters = flow_func.validate_parameters(parameters=parameters)
                 ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
  File "/Users/patrick/.virtualenvs/infrahub/lib/python3.12/site-packages/prefect/flows.py", line 652, in validate_parameters
    return validated_fn.validate_call_args(args, kwargs)
           ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
  File "/Users/patrick/.virtualenvs/infrahub/lib/python3.12/site-packages/prefect/_internal/pydantic/validated_func.py", line 297, in validate_call_args
    validated = self.model.model_validate(values)
                ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
  File "/Users/patrick/.virtualenvs/infrahub/lib/python3.12/site-packages/pydantic/main.py", line 627, in model_validate
    return cls.__pydantic_validator__.validate_python(
           ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
  File "/Users/patrick/.virtualenvs/infrahub/lib/python3.12/site-packages/pydantic/_internal/_mock_val_ser.py", line 100, in __getattr__
    raise PydanticUserError(self._error_message, code=self._code)
pydantic.errors.PydanticUserError: `Create_BranchModel` is not fully defined; you should define `BranchCreateModel`, then call `Create_BranchModel.model_rebuild()`.
```

The issue seems to be that a models is dynamically generated here:

https://github.com/PrefectHQ/prefect/blob/3.4.25/src/prefect/_internal/pydantic/validated_func.py#L151-L227

Then within this context the models we're sending in as parameters doesn't exist. If I add these inline imports at the top of the `_create_model()` method it works.

```python
        from infrahub.context import InfrahubContext
        from infrahub.graphql.mutations.models import BranchCreateModel
```

The above is of course just an example to highlight the issue.

Likewise if I rebuild the model and instead end `_create_model()` with this it also works and could be a fix:

```python
        self.model.model_rebuild(_types_namespace=self.raw_function.__globals__)
```

I'm not completely sure about the overhead of this when, i.e. how resource intensive that `.model_rebuild` is or the impact of always creating a new model on the fly.

### Version info

```Text

Version:              3.4.25
API version:          0.8.4
Python version:       3.12.11
Git commit:           8a37e7b1
Built:                Thu, Oct 23, 2025 07:58 PM
OS/Arch:              darwin/arm64
Profile:              local
Server type:          server
Pydantic version:     2.10.6
Server:
  Database:           postgresql
  PostgreSQL version: 16.10
Integrations:
  prefect-client:     3.4.25
  prefect-redis:      0.2.6
```

_No response_

```
  File "/usr/local/lib/python3.12/site-packages/sqlalchemy/dialects/postgresql/asyncpg.py", line 792, in _handle_exception
    raise translated_error from error
sqlalchemy.exc.DBAPIError: (sqlalchemy.dialects.postgresql.asyncpg.Error) <class 'asyncpg.exceptions.CharacterNotInRepertoireError'>: invalid byte sequence for encoding "UTF8": 0x00
```

I would expect the prefect server to sanitize the input before sending it to the DB, maybe by calling `str.decode('utf-8', errors='replace')`

prefecthq/prefect#19301.body:
## Summary

We discovered a regression in interval schedules after the pendulum → `datetime/zoneinfo` refactor. When the schedule JSON contains a fixed-offset anchor such as `"2024-07-01T01:15:00+09:00"` alongside a non-UTC timezone like `"Asia/Tokyo"`, the scheduler drifts runs by the offset—so 01:15 JST became 10:15 JST.

## Changes

The culprit is the new tzinfo "normalization." For tzinfos that aren't `ZoneInfo`, the code called `.tzname()` to get a timezone name; offset tzinfos return strings like `"UTC+09:00"`, which fails when trying to create a `ZoneInfo` from that invalid key.

The fix uses `.astimezone(ZoneInfo(target_timezone))` for offset-based tzinfos, which preserves both the absolute instant and microsecond precision. Naive datetimes still assume the schedule's timezone, and true `ZoneInfo` objects continue down the existing `.to_tz()` path.

## Testing

Added regression test `test_offset_anchor_preserves_local_hour` which:
- Only runs on Python 3.13+ (where the bug exists)
- Creates an interval schedule with offset-based anchor and named timezone
- Verifies scheduled times preserve the local hour instead of drifting

Test confirmed:
- ❌ Fails on main (ZoneInfoNotFoundError: 'No time zone found with key UTC+09:00')
- ✅ Passes with fix

All existing interval schedule tests and scheduler service tests pass on Python 3.13.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
prefecthq/prefect#19301.title: Fix time zone normalization bug in interval schedules

prefecthq/prefect#19317.body:
### Bug summary

**Setup:** self-hosted, HA, multiple API instances, single “server services” instance, Redis + Postgres

**Symptom:** `prefect.automation.triggered` appears (UI/DB/Redis), entry lands in Redis stream `actions` and is consumed (pending=0), but:
- no `prefect.automation.action.executed` / `...failed`
- deployment not started
- message ends up in `dlq:*`
- **no error in pod logs**

**Root cause:** actions service uses `prefect.server.api.clients.OrchestrationClient`, which calls `create_app()` → `create_ui_static_subpath()` and tries to create:
`/usr/local/lib/python3.12/site-packages/prefect/server/ui_build`
→ in read-only directory (rootless container) this raises `PermissionError`
→ exception is swallowed, message goes to DLQ.

**Workaround:** set on the services pod:
```env
PREFECT_UI_STATIC_DIRECTORY=/writable/dir
```

**Ask**: log the actual exception before DLQ; skip/relax UI creation for background/actions services.

prefecthq/prefect#19326.body:
### Bug summary

Calling a task with incorrect argument types results in an incorrect error shown by pyright. Minimal reproducible code and error are shown below. 

```python
from prefect import task

@task
async def f(
    s: str
) -> None: ...

async def test_correct_arg_type():
    await f("")
    # No Pyright Errors

async def test_wrong_arg_type():
    await f(None)
    # test.py:13:11 - error: "State[CoroutineType[Any, Any, None]]" is not awaitable
    #  "State[CoroutineType[Any, Any, None]]" is incompatible with protocol "Awaitable[_T_co@Awaitable]"
    #   "__await__" is not present (reportGeneralTypeIssues)
```

### Bug summary

Running a flow with a task within the Prefect test harness fails the second time if run twice. For example:

```python
from prefect import flow, task
from prefect.testing.utilities import prefect_test_harness

@task
def example_task():
    pass

@flow
def example_flow():
    example_task()

with prefect_test_harness():
    example_flow()

with prefect_test_harness():
    example_flow()
```

prefecthq/prefect#19348.body:
### Bug summary

In Prefect 3.4.18, I could deploy a flow with a trigger that used Jinja-templated Prefect variables in prefect.yaml

```
deployments:
  - name: deployment_name
    description: Example trigger using Prefect variables
    entrypoint: flows/example.py:my_flow
    parameters:
      workspace_name: PROD
      upload_file: true
    triggers:
      - type: event
        enabled: "{{ prefect.variables.is_prod }}"
        match_related:
          prefect.resource.role: deployment
          prefect.resource.name: other_deployment
        expect:
          - prefect.flow-run.Completed
```

Run:
```
prefect deploy --prefect-file prefect.yaml --name deployment_name --no-prompt
```

After upgrading to Prefect 3.5.0, running prefect deploy fails validation with:
```
Invalid fields in deployments:
  • deployment_name: triggers

For valid deployment fields and examples, go to: https://docs.prefect.io/v3/concepts/deployments#deployment-schema
```

**Expected behavior**

Jinja templating (for {{ prefect.variables.is_prod }}) should resolve before validation, or
the error should explicitly say that enabled must be a boolean rather than marking the whole
triggers field invalid.

prefecthq/prefect#19359.body:
## Description

When `run_deployment` is called from within a task, the resulting deployment flow run appears separately from the triggering task in the execution graph. This differs from the behavior of local subflows, which correctly roll up under their triggering task.

## Reproduction

```python
from prefect import flow, task
from prefect.deployments import run_deployment

@flow
def parent_flow(num_subflows: int) -> None:
    trigger_subflows.map([_ for _ in range(num_subflows)])

@task
def trigger_subflows(x: int) -> None:
    # Local subflow - correctly appears under the task in execution graph
    subflow(x=x)
    
    # Deployment - appears separate from the task in execution graph
    # run_deployment('subflow/subflow-deployment', parameters=dict(x=x))

@flow
def subflow(x: int) -> None:
    print(f'Running subflow with x={x}')
```

**With local subflow call**: The subflow correctly appears as a child of the `trigger_subflows` task in the execution graph.

**With `run_deployment` call**: The deployment flow run appears disconnected from the `trigger_subflows` task in the execution graph.

## Expected Behavior

When `run_deployment` is called from within a task, the resulting flow run should appear as a child of that task in the execution graph, similar to how local subflows behave.

## Actual Behavior

The deployment flow run appears separately/disconnected from the triggering task in the execution graph, making it difficult to trace which task triggered which deployment run.

## Technical Context

`run_deployment` was created before tasks could be nested within each other and wasn't updated to be fully aware of the current task run context when that feature was added. The function does create a parent task run and links it via `parent_task_run_id` (see `src/prefect/deployments/flow_runs.py:163-170`), but the parent task run may not be properly registered with the current flow run context for execution graph visibility.
prefecthq/prefect#19359.title: run_deployment doesn't appear under triggering task in execution graph

prefecthq/prefect#19360.body:
## current behavior

in `src/prefect/server/database/configurations.py:38`, we check:

```python
if os.getenv(PREFECT_LOGFIRE_ENABLED):
    import logfire
```

this is truthy for ANY string value, including `"0"` or `"false"`. so setting `PREFECT_LOGFIRE_ENABLED=0` still tries to import logfire.

## expected behavior

should check explicitly for truthy values:

```python
if os.getenv(PREFECT_LOGFIRE_ENABLED) == "1":
    import logfire
```

or use proper boolean parsing like pydantic settings does.

## impact

this makes it impossible to disable logfire via environment variable override in deployment scenarios where env vars are inherited (e.g., kubernetes helm charts).

discovered while trying to disable logfire for database migrations that inherit `PREFECT_LOGFIRE_ENABLED=1` from server config but don't have logfire installed.
prefecthq/prefect#19360.title: PREFECT_LOGFIRE_ENABLED env var check is not properly handling false values

prefecthq/prefect#19367.body:
### Bug summary

Utilizing tags on tasks results in repeated calls to concurrency endpoints, this is necessary at the moment to determine if a concurrency limit exists but can result in repeated unnecessary calls to the api, and if using prefect cloud can trigger errors related to rate limits unexpectedly when applying tags to tasks as we're also going to attempt to acquire a concurrency slot even if a concurrency limit for the tag doesn't exist.

Relevant code https://github.com/PrefectHQ/prefect/blob/87f5e2e1d065c46f61846639d1a83d9e343850f1/src/prefect/task_engine.py#L829

### Version info

prefecthq/prefect#19393.body:
### Describe the current behavior

Currently when bumping Prefect to 3.6.0, uv failed with https://github.com/hongbo-miao/hongbomiao.com/pull/35341

```sh
Command failed: uv lock --upgrade-package prefect --upgrade-package prefect-client
Using CPython 3.14.0 interpreter at: /opt/containerbase/tools/python/3.14.0/bin/python3.14
warning: No `requires-python` value found in the workspace. Defaulting to `>=3.14`.
  × No solution found when resolving dependencies:
  ╰─▶ Because only pydocket<0.13.0b2 is available and prefect==3.6.0 depends
      on pydocket>=0.13.0b2, we can conclude that prefect==3.6.0 cannot be
      used.
      And because prefect-greet:dev depends on prefect==3.6.0 and your
      project requires prefect-greet:dev, we can conclude that your project's
      requirements are unsatisfiable.

      hint: `pydocket` was requested with a pre-release marker (e.g.,
      pydocket>=0.13.0b2), but pre-releases weren't enabled (try:
      `--prerelease=allow`)
```

Because Prefect depends on `pydocket>=0.13.0b2`, and pydocket does not have a stable version beyond this version.

### Describe the proposed behavior

Just a suggestion, it would be great to avoid depending on pre-release dependencies for stability in production, thanks! ☺️
prefecthq/prefect#19393.title: Would be great to avoid depending on pre-release dependencies

prefecthq/prefect#19415.body:
### Description
After deploying a flow linked to a work queue, the work_queue_id is empty, though work_queue_name shows the correct value.

### Reproduction
1. Create a deployment:
```
name: backup_uploader
description: "Nightly backup uploader flow (UAT & PROD)"
entrypoint: "prefect_backup_uploader.py:backup_uploader_flow"
version: 1.0.0

deployments:
  - name: "backup_uploader_uat"
    description: "UAT environment nightly backup uploader"
    work_pool:
      name: "gos"
      work_queue_name: "uat"
    parameters:
      env: "uat"
    schedule:
      cron: "22 3 * * *"
      timezone: "America/Regina"
```
2. Start worker:
`prefect worker start --pool "gos" --work-queue "uat"`
3. Deploy the deployment via prefect deploy.
4. Check the deployment using `prefect deployment inspect backup_uploader_flow/backup_uploader_uat`
5. Deployment is not assigned to the queue correctly:
```
    'work_queue_name': 'uat',
    'last_polled': '2025-11-10T10:02:42.440443Z',
    'parameter_openapi_schema': {'type': 'object', 'title': 'Parameters', 'properties': {'env': {'type': 'string', 'title': 'env', 'default': 'dev', 'position': 0}}},
    'path': None,
    'entrypoint': 'prefect_backup_uploader.py:run_backup_uploader_flow',
    'storage_document_id': None,
    'infrastructure_document_id': None,
    'created_by': {'id': 'ae67e097-1057-41f9-9fe7-11d045b8e77a', 'type': 'USER', 'display_value': 'zeta2-tucanae-anomaly'},
    'updated_by': {'id': 'ae67e097-1057-41f9-9fe7-11d045b8e77a', 'type': 'USER', 'display_value': 'zeta2-tucanae-anomaly'},
    'work_queue_id': None,
```

prefecthq/prefect#19435.body:
## current behavior

calling `pause_flow_run()` from within a task raises `RuntimeError: Cannot pause task runs.`

```python
from prefect import flow, task
from prefect.flow_runs import pause_flow_run
from prefect.input import RunInput

class ApprovalInput(RunInput):
    approved: bool

@task
async def get_approval():
    approval = await pause_flow_run(
        wait_for_input=ApprovalInput
    )
    return approval.approved

@flow
async def my_flow():
    approved = await get_approval()
    print(f"approved: {approved}")
```

error:
```
RuntimeError: Cannot pause task runs.
```

## expected behavior

`pause_flow_run()` should pause the parent flow run, not the task run. the function is specifically named `pause_flow_run()` not `pause_task_run()`, so it seems reasonable to expect it would pause the flow regardless of where it's called from.

## use case

encapsulating approval logic in a reusable task that can handle different approval types (human via pause, ai via agent, etc.)

## workaround

make the approval function a flow instead of a task, but this feels less idiomatic since it's not really a "flow" conceptually - it's a step in a larger flow.
prefecthq/prefect#19435.title: pause_flow_run() raises 'Cannot pause task runs' when called from within a task

prefecthq/prefect#19438.body:
### Describe the current behavior

### Description:

It would be great if the DatabricksCredentials block supported authenticating with a service principal using a client_id and client_secret.

At the moment, the block only allows authentication using a personal access token (PAT), which is tied to an individual user. For production or automated environments, service principal authentication is preferred as it aligns with enterprise security practices and avoids dependency on user accounts.

### Current Behaviour

Currently, Databricks credentials in Prefect require a user PAT:

```python
from prefect_databricks import DatabricksCredentials

creds = DatabricksCredentials(
    databricks_instance="https://<workspace>.cloud.databricks.com",
    token="<personal_access_token>"
)

```



### Describe the proposed behavior

Add support for authenticating with:

```python
DatabricksCredentials(
    databricks_instance="https://<workspace>.cloud.databricks.com",
    client_id="<service_principal_id>",
    client_secret="<service_principal_secret>",
    tenant_id="<tenant_id>"  # optional, for Azure Databricks
)
```
This would use Databricks’ supported OAuth 2.0 flow for service principals instead of a PAT.


### Example Use

_No response_

### Additional context

### Why this matters

- Enables secure, automated orchestration in Databricks without relying on user-scoped tokens.
- Aligns with enterprise IAM standards for least privilege and rotation policies.
- Reduces operational overhead for teams managing service accounts.
- Service principal–based authentication is Databricks’ recommended approach for production systems.

References
	•	[Databricks OAuth machine-to-machine (M2M) authentication docs](https://docs.databricks.com/en/dev-tools/auth/oauth-m2m.html)￼
	•	[Databricks: Use service principals to authenticate](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/databricks/dev-tools/service-principals)￼
	•	[Databricks REST API authentication overview](https://docs.databricks.com/en/dev-tools/api/latest/authentication.html)￼
prefecthq/prefect#19438.title: Support Databricks authentication using Service Principal (client ID and secret)

If you change the order and put prefect flow at the end, then it works:

```python
from __future__ import annotations
from prefect import flow
from pydantic import BaseModel, Field

class A(BaseModel):
    a: B = Field()


class B(BaseModel):
    b: str = Field()

@flow
def main(a:A):
```

Or if you dont use prefect flow at all:

```python
from __future__ import annotations
from prefect import flow

class A(BaseModel):

class B(BaseModel):

a = A(a=B(b="asdf"))
```

So the problem is, that python 3.12 and pydantic by itself work well with forward references, but prefect doesnt handle it well.

prefecthq/prefect#19449.body:
### Bug summary

When I have a `Flow` running inside a `Task` inside another `Flow`, it inherits the parent's attributes even if it has its own attributes set. See example with `log_prints`:

```python
from prefect import flow, task

@flow(log_prints=False)
def flow1():
    print('This should not be logged!') # <- This is logged!

@flow(log_prints=True)
def flow2():
    print('This should be logged!')
    task(flow1)()

if __name__ == "__main__":
    flow2()
```

prefecthq/prefect#19467.body:
### Bug summary

After upgrading to 3.6.1 from 3.4, when creating a deployment through the cli with a yaml defined deployment, I am getting a yaml validation error for a previously working deployment, but there is no detail on the error.

Reproducing with a simple deployment:
```yaml
name: example-deployment
prefect-version: 3 # This is the source of the validation error

pull:
  - prefect.deployments.steps.git_clone:
      id: clone-step-scripts
      repository: https://github.com/me/myrepo.git
      branch: main
      credentials: '{{ prefect.blocks.github-credentials.my-credentials }}'
deployments:
  - name: example-deployment
    description: Test flow deployed from github with external dependencies
    entrypoint: flow.py:test_flow
    work_pool:
      name: my-pool
```
Flow file is a hello world 
```python
from prefect import flow

@flow(log_prints=True)
def test_flow():
    print("hello world")
```

Running deploy with this file
```sh
prefect --no-prompt deploy --prefect-file prefect.yaml
```
Produces an error that looks like
```
Validation error in config file

Skipping deployment configuration due to validation errors.
An entrypoint must be provided:

        prefect deploy path/to/file.py:flow_function

You can also provide an entrypoint in a prefect.yaml file.
```

This is confusing! My entry point looks fine! So peeling back the deployment validation in the deploy cli I find the error

```
from prefect.cli.deploy._config import _load_deploy_configs_and_actions

# The function that handles config validation returns empty data
cfg = Path("prefect.yaml")
_load_deploy_configs_and_actions(cfg) 
>>> Validation error in config file
>>>
>>> Skipping deployment configuration due to validation errors.
>>> Out[27]: ([], {'build': [], 'push': [], 'pull': []})

# The Pydantic model for the yaml config does show the error
import yaml
from pathlib import Path
from prefect.cli.deploy._models import PrefectYamlModel

with cfg.open() as f:
    data = yaml.safe_load(f)
PrefectYamlModel.model_validate(data)
>>> ValidationError: 1 validation error for PrefectYamlModel
>>> prefect-version
>>>  Input should be a valid string [type=string_type, input_value=3, input_type=int]
>>>    For further information visit https://errors.pydantic.dev/2.12/v/string_type

# Great! I see now that the prefect-version field needs to be forced to a string in the yaml
```

I am not certain if this is a unique issue with validation of the top level values for `prefect-version` / `name` or applies to all fields.

Since this was introduced between 3.4 and 3.6.1, I am guessing the issue is probably in this commit which changed the CLI stdout for validation errors https://github.com/PrefectHQ/prefect/commit/5e31ddaf65fde592df82e322bbd4ce084fc15467

This exception handling is not correctly logging the error context
https://github.com/PrefectHQ/prefect/blob/6a68cfff794e700d85800476c38a733eb61d1aae/src/prefect/cli/deploy/_config.py#L122-L132



### Version info

```Text
Version:              3.6.1
API version:          0.8.4
Python version:       3.13.5
Git commit:           9b54529a
Built:                Mon, Nov 10, 2025 05:33 PM
OS/Arch:              linux/x86_64
Profile:              harmonia-prod
Server type:          server
Pydantic version:     2.12.4
Server:
  Database:           sqlite
  SQLite version:     3.47.1
```

### Additional context

I am running the cli primarily using `uv tool run`

prefecthq/prefect#19483.body:
several CLI commands produce table or list output but don't support `--output json` / `-o json` for machine-readable output. the pattern already exists in commands like `flow-run ls`, `deployment ls`, `artifact ls`, and `variable ls` — each gap just needs to follow the same approach.

## how to contribute

pick an unchecked command from the list below, open a PR, and reference this issue (e.g. "related to #19483"). no need to create a child issue — just claim it in a comment here and open the PR.

the existing implementations in `flow-run ls` and `deployment ls` are good references for the pattern:
- add `--output` / `-o` option (accepts `"json"`)
- when `output == "json"`, serialize the response objects with `orjson.dumps(..., option=orjson.OPT_INDENT_2)` and print
- when not set, render the existing Rich table as before
- add tests for both output modes

## already done

- [x] `flow-run ls` — #19484

- [x] `block ls` — #20195

prefecthq/prefect#19501.body:
### Bug summary

Using prefect 3.6.3 to deploy a flow from prefect.yaml with jinja template trigger definitions prefect 3.6.3 the parameters are empty in the resulting automation action. With prefect 3.6.2 the automation action contains the parameters as expected.

flow.py:
```python
from prefect import flow


@flow
def say_hello(name: str) -> str:
    return f"Hello, {name}!"
```

prefect.yaml:
```yaml
deployments:
  - name: say-hello
    entrypoint: flow.py:say_hello
    work_pool:
      name: my-work-pool
    triggers:
      - type: event
        enabled: true
        match:
          prefect.resource.id: hello.world
        expect:
          - external.resource.pinged
        parameters:
          name: "{{ event.name }}"
```

Prefect 3.6.2 works as expected - i.e. `parameters={'name': '{{ event.name }}'}`:
```bash
$ uvx prefect@3.6.2 --no-prompt deploy --prefect-file prefect.yaml --all
$ uvx prefect@3.6.2 automation inspect say-hello__automation_1
[
    Automation(
        name='say-hello__automation_1',
        tags=[],
        trigger=EventTrigger(
            match=ResourceSpecification(root={'prefect.resource.id': 'hello.world'}),
            match_related=ResourceSpecification(root={}),
            after=set(),
            expect={'external.resource.pinged'},
            for_each=set()
        ),
        actions=[
            RunDeployment(
                deployment_id=UUID('fc5ab915-d4ca-4c07-bbea-31859d2fa082'),
                parameters={'name': '{{ event.name }}'},
                schedule_after=datetime.timedelta(0)
            )
        ],
        actions_on_trigger=[],
        actions_on_resolve=[],
        id='f0c33e73-5bc7-4123-a9f8-bf156e5c964e'
    )
]
```

With prefect 3.6.3 the parameters are empty - i.e. `parameters={}`:
```bash
$ uvx prefect@3.6.3 --no-prompt deploy --prefect-file prefect.yaml --all
$ uvx prefect@3.6.3 automation inspect say-hello__automation_1
[
    Automation(
        name='say-hello__automation_1',
        tags=[],
        trigger=EventTrigger(
            match=ResourceSpecification(root={'prefect.resource.id': 'hello.world'}),
            match_related=ResourceSpecification(root={}),
            after=set(),
            expect={'external.resource.pinged'},
            for_each=set()
        ),
        actions=[RunDeployment(deployment_id=UUID('fc5ab915-d4ca-4c07-bbea-31859d2fa082'), parameters={}, schedule_after=datetime.timedelta(0))],
        actions_on_trigger=[],
        actions_on_resolve=[],
        id='b57cb8d3-dd49-4897-ab68-b8e50e1c05bb'
    )
]
```

### Version info

```Text
$ uvx prefect@3.6.3 version
Version:              3.6.3
API version:          0.8.4
Python version:       3.13.1
Git commit:           dbc96d7c
Built:                Wed, Nov 19, 2025 10:19 PM
OS/Arch:              darwin/arm64
Profile:              prefect-cloud
Server type:          cloud
Pydantic version:     2.12.4
Server:

  SQLite version:     3.47.1
```

### Additional context

The [release notes for Prefect 3.6.3](https://github.com/PrefectHQ/prefect/releases/tag/3.6.3) include:
> fix: validate deployment triggers after templating so jinja-based booleans work, should close https://github.com/PrefectHQ/prefect/issues/19348 by [@iclectic](https://github.com/iclectic) in https://github.com/PrefectHQ/prefect/pull/19414
which seems a likely culprit for this regression.

### Bug summary

Related to #19419 I have the problem, that special characters in username/password/token also fail when starting a deployment, that includes a pull step depending on these in authentication.
My mwe looks as follows:

```python
# hello.py
from prefect import flow, task
# import pandas as pd


@task
def create_dataframe(name: str) -> dict:
    data = {"greeting": [f"Hello, {name}!"], "length": [len(name) + 7]}
    # df = pd.DataFrame(data)
    return data


@flow(log_prints=True)
def hello(name: str = "world"):
    df = create_dataframe(name)
    print(df)


if __name__ == "__main__":
    hello.deploy(
        name="hello-on-docker",
        work_pool_name="my-docker-pool",
        image="prefect-hello:local",  # local tag
        push=False,  # <- don't push to Docker Hub
        job_variables={"pip_packages": ["pandas", "prefect-aws"]},
    )
```

This flow I then want to deploy to a prefect server running image version `3.6-python3.10-conda` with a single docker worker named `my-docker-worker`.
Currently I am having to do a workaround like in the following snippet. 
Note that the comments include variants I tried, which all failed.

```python
import os
from prefect import flow
from pydantic import SecretStr, field_validator
from typing import Optional
from urllib.parse import urlparse, urlunparse
import re


from prefect_bitbucket.credentials import BitBucketCredentials, _quote_credential
from prefect.runner.storage import GitRepository


class FixedBitbucketCredentials(BitBucketCredentials):
    """A subclass of BitBucketCredentials that works around the url formatting issues.
    By allowing already url escaped characters '%' to be saved as a block, such that the pull step  
    """

    @field_validator("username")
    def _validate_username(cls, value: str) -> str:
        """Allow common special characters used in Bitbucket usernames, such as email addresses."""
        # This is a hacky way to allow already quoted usernames
        pattern = r"^[A-Za-z0-9@._+%-]+$"

        if not re.match(pattern, value):
            raise ValueError(
                "Username contains invalid characters. Allowed: letters, numbers, @ . _ + % -"
            )
        if len(value) > 100:
            raise ValueError("Username cannot be longer than 100 characters.")
        return value

    def format_git_credentials(self, url: str) -> str:
        """
        Format and return the full git URL with BitBucket credentials embedded.

        BitBucket has different authentication formats:
        - BitBucket Server: username:token format required
        - BitBucket Cloud: x-token-auth:token prefix

        Args:
            url: Repository URL (e.g., "https://bitbucket.org/org/repo.git")

        Returns:
            Complete URL with credentials embedded

        Raises:
            ValueError: If credentials are not properly configured
        """
        parsed = urlparse(url)

        # Get the token (prefer token field, fall back to password)
        token_value: Optional[str] = None
        if self.token:
            token_value = self.token.get_secret_value()
        elif self.password:
            token_value = self.password.get_secret_value()

        if not token_value:
            raise ValueError(
                "Token or password is required for BitBucket authentication"
            )

        # BitBucket Server requires username:token format
        if "bitbucketserver" in parsed.netloc:
            if not self.username:
                raise ValueError(
                    "Username is required for BitBucket Server authentication"
                )
            credentials = f"{self.username}:{token_value}"
        # BitBucket Cloud uses x-token-auth: prefix
        # If token already has a colon or prefix, use as-is
        elif ":" in token_value:
            # Split and encode each part separately
            parts = token_value.split(":", 1)
            credentials = f"{parts[0]}:{parts[1]}"
        elif self.username and self.password:
            # if username and password are provided, use basic auth, regardless of netloc
            # the issue is that bitbucketserver in netloc is too narrow a check
            # to try username:password auth
            credentials = f"{self.username}:{token_value}"
        else:
            credentials = f"x-token-auth:{token_value}"

        # Insert credentials into URL
        return urlunparse(parsed._replace(netloc=f"{credentials}@{parsed.netloc}"))


if __name__ == "__main__":
    # Workaround: save quoted username/pw here, because otherwise
    # the deployed flow fails in pull step, since it doesn't use `format_git_credentials`
    # from `BitBucketCredentials` class fixed in #19419 

    # A real fix would probably to include the quoting in the pull steps url formatting as well
    # Normal `BitBucketCredentials` will allow to create the deployment, but will fail when running the flow
    creds = FixedBitbucketCredentials(
        username=_quote_credential(os.getenv("USER")), # contains '@'
        password=SecretStr(_quote_credential(os.getenv("PW"))), # contains e.g. '!'
    )
    creds.save(
        "my-bitbucket-creds", overwrite=True
    )  # needed to set _block_document_name, can't pass directly to GitRepository

    creds_loaded = FixedBitbucketCredentials.load("my-bitbucket-creds")
    # This doesn't work, because it's not a `RunnerStorage` and will fail in this script when trying to access its `_block_document_name`
    # source = BitBucketRepository(
    #     repository="corporate_bitbucket_without_'bitbucketserver'_in_netloc",
    #     reference="hello_world",
    #     bitbucket_credentials=creds_loaded,
    # )

    source_git = GitRepository(
        url="corporate_bitbucket_without_'bitbucketserver'_in_netloc",
        branch="hello_world",
        credentials=FixedBitbucketCredentials.load("my-bitbucket-creds"),
    )

    # Point to the flow function inside your repo
    flow.from_source(
        source=source_git,
        entrypoint="hello.py:hello",
    ).deploy(
        name="hello-from-bitbucket",
        work_pool_name="my-docker-pool",
        build=False, 
    )

```

So to summarize there are two issues. One is that `'bitbucketserver' in netloc` is too narrow a check to try username password clones in my special case and second is that the url formatting in `GitRepository` is also unaware of escaping special characters.

prefecthq/prefect#19526.body:
### Describe the current behavior

I am trying to create an automation, reporting on crashed flows.
In `prefect.yaml` I am able to template out most of the required fields in order to create a deep link to the event in question. However, prefect cloud requires a date passed in link:
```
[View Event](https://app.prefect.cloud/account/{{ event.account }}/workspace/{{ event.workspace }}/events/event/{{ event.occurred }}/{{ event.id }})
```
will fail, as the event.occured is a full timestamp, but the link needs to be only the date e.g. `2025-11-24`

### Describe the proposed behavior

Multiple options:
- Allow the "date" part of the URL to be passed verbatim (full timestamp as reported by the event)
- New URL endpoint, omitting the date, only taking the ID as input
- Add a new event field `self.url` we can template on.  

### Example Use

Report failures to other systems and provide any track-back

### Additional context

_No response_
prefecthq/prefect#19526.title: Add ability to link to events without parsing date strings

prefecthq/prefect#19527.body:
### Describe the current behavior

I deploy a number of flows together on a static infrastructure from python using `serve(deployment_1, deployment_2, ..., deployment_n)` with `deployment_x` being the output of `flow_x.to_deployment(...)`.

I'd like the persisted results of the flow runs from different flows to be separated. Because they're deployed at the same time, I can't use `PREFECT_RESULTS_LOCAL_STORAGE_PATH` for this purpose. Since I don't deploy to a work pool, it seems I can't use `job_variables` to set different environment variables on deployments either.

Now, I can use the `result_storage` input parameter in the `flow` decorator. However, for this I need to explicitly create a `LocalFilesystem` block and load it. I find this rather convoluted for a fairly simple need, especially considering that creating a block myself is not necessary when setting a custom local storage location through the `PREFECT_RESULTS_LOCAL_STORAGE_PATH` environment variable.

### Describe the proposed behavior

I don't know if there are reasons for this asymmetry, but I would argue that being allowed to pass a `Path` object (to distinguish it from a block name string) to `result_storage` to change the default location would be a reasonable thing to expect from a user's perspective. In any case, I'd like setting a custom storage path with `result_storage` to be as simple as it is with the environment variable, if that's possible.

### Example Use

```python
@flow(result_storage=Path(path1)):
def flow1():
   # flow code

@flow(result_storage=Path(path2)):
def flow2():
    # flow code
```

### Additional context

_No response_
prefecthq/prefect#19527.title: Make it easy to define custom local result storage location on a per-flow basis from python

prefecthq/prefect#19627.body: ### Bug summary ### Setup * ECS * EC2 Compatibility (our jobs need > 128GB of Ram which renders Fargate ineligible) * ECS Capacity Provider with EC2 Auto Scaling Group ** desired size is 0 as our jobs run for 2 hours daily and we want 0 instances up when no jobs are running * Capacity Provider is set as default Capacity Provider in AWS ### The Problem * when the AWS task is run with `launchType=EC2` (which is technically correct) the Capacity Provider will not be triggered and AWS returns with an immediate error `No Container Instances were found in your cluster.` (see screenshot 1). This then gets propagated by Prefect: [Prefect Github Code](https://github.com/PrefectHQ/prefect/blob/290d70f7a8c52baf71661c29d9319e8dd2423a2e/src/integrations/prefect-aws/prefect_aws/workers/ecs_worker.py#L851-L859). * the `launchType` is defaulted to EC2 by the work pool template (see screenshot 2) ### Attempted Solution * when running the task definition by hand using boto3 (see screenshot 1 with commented out `launchType="EC2"`) the task gets successfully submitted to AWS and is marked as pending -> the capacity provider then spins up resources and once done the task starts running * Hence I set the `launchType` to `null` in the job variables section (see screenshot 3). ### What I Believe is a Bug * Above attempted solution triggers: `Error Flow run could not be submitted to infrastructure: 'requiresCompatibilities'`. I traced this to [this prefect code](https://github.com/PrefectHQ/prefect/blob/290d70f7a8c52baf71661c29d9319e8dd2423a2e/src/integrations/prefect-aws/prefect_aws/workers/ecs_worker.py#L977-L986) which forces the `requiresCompatibilities` to be `FARGATE` if the `launchType` is not explicitly set to `EC2`. * To my understanding, in order to properly trigger the ECS EC2 Capacity Provider the launch type must NOT be set, but prefect does not allow any permutation of config that produces these parameters for the AWS task definition and `run_task` invocation. Hoping I explained this well, it's took me quite some time digging through this. Looking forward to responses :-) **Screenshot 1:** (manually located the prefect task definition and fired it off) **Screenshot 2:** Work pool launch type default ** Screenshot 3:** Setting lauch type to null ### Version info ```Text Version: 3.4.1 API version: 0.8.4 Python version: 3.12.4 Git commit: b47ad8e1 Built: Thu, May 08, 2025 08:42 PM OS/Arch: darwin/arm64 Profile: default Server type: cloud Pydantic version: 2.11.4 ``` ### Additional context _No response_
prefecthq/prefect#19627.title: AWS ECS EC2 Work Pool Fails with Capacity Provider Strategy

prefecthq/prefect#19644.body:
### Describe the current behavior

The [AzureBlobStorageCredentials](https://github.com/PrefectHQ/prefect/blob/dd875ab53c3aec6a5520345544a7048ab6c08817/src/integrations/prefect-azure/prefect_azure/credentials.py#L75) block currently supports authentication using connection strings and account URLs only. [Push/pull steps](https://github.com/PrefectHQ/prefect/blob/dd875ab53c3aec6a5520345544a7048ab6c08817/src/integrations/prefect-azure/prefect_azure/deployments/steps.py#L90-L94) for Azure Blob Storage do not allow [Service Principal Name (SPN) authentication](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/entra/identity-platform/app-objects-and-service-principals?tabs=browser#service-principal-object).
Other blocks like [AzureMlCredentials](https://github.com/PrefectHQ/prefect/blob/dd875ab53c3aec6a5520345544a7048ab6c08817/src/integrations/prefect-azure/prefect_azure/credentials.py#L397) and [AzureContainerInstanceCredentials](https://github.com/PrefectHQ/prefect/blob/dd875ab53c3aec6a5520345544a7048ab6c08817/src/integrations/prefect-azure/prefect_azure/credentials.py#L485) support SPN, but their usage is limited and does not extend to blob storage operations.

### Describe the proposed behavior

Add SPN authentication as a supported method in `AzureBlobStorageCredentials`. Enable SPN-based authentication for pull steps (both push/pull) and other blob storage operations.
Ensure consistency with other Azure integrations that already support SPN.

### Example Use

Example: Using SPN in pull steps with a pre-existing blob-storage-credentials block
```yaml
build: null

push:
- prefect_azure.deployments.steps.push_to_azure_blob_storage:
    id: push_code
    requires: prefect-azure>=0.4
    container: my-prefect-azure-container
    folder: my-folder
    credentials: "{{ prefect.blocks.azure-blob-storage-credentials }}" 
    # SPN-based authentication block

pull:
- prefect_azure.deployments.steps.pull_from_azure_blob_storage:
    id: pull_code
    requires: prefect-azure>=0.4
    container: '{{ push_code.container }}'
    folder: '{{ push_code.folder }}'
    credentials: "{{ prefect.blocks.azure-blob-storage-credentials }}" 
    # SPN-based authentication block

deployments:
- name: my-azure-deployment
  version: null
  tags: []
  concurrency_limit: null
  description: null
  entrypoint: my_file.py:my_flow
  parameters: {}
  work_pool:
    name: my-work-pool
    work_queue_name: null
    job_variables: {}
  enforce_parameter_schema: true
  schedules: []
```

Example: Creating SPN Credentials Block in Python
```python
from prefect.blocks.system import Secret
from prefect_azure.credentials import AzureBlobStorageCredentials

# Create SPN-based credentials block
spn_credentials = AzureBlobStorageCredentials(
    tenant_id="your-tenant-id",
    client_id="your-client-id",
    client_secret=Secret.load("your-client-secret-block"),
    account_url="https://<your-storage-account>.blob.core.windows.net"
)


# Save the block for reuse
spn_credentials.save("my-spn-credentials-block", overwrite=True)
```

### Additional context

Enterprise environments often require SPN for secure and standardized access. Improves security and compliance by reducing reliance on connection strings. Aligns blob storage integration with other Azure services in Prefect.
prefecthq/prefect#19644.title: Add SPN Authentication Support for AzureBlobStorageCredentials block

prefecthq/prefect#19658.body:
## Bug summary

Workers with `--with-healthcheck` fail to spawn subprocesses when the user has `uvicorn[standard]<=0.35.0` installed (which includes uvloop).

## Root cause

**uvicorn ≤0.35.0** calls `uvloop_setup()` when starting, which sets a **global** `uvloop.EventLoopPolicy()`. When the worker then tries to spawn a subprocess using a `SelectorEventLoop`, it fails because uvloop's policy doesn't implement `get_child_watcher()`.

**uvicorn ≥0.36.0** switched to using `loop_factory` which doesn't modify the global policy.

## Reproduction

```bash
docker run --rm python:3.13-slim bash -c "
pip install -q prefect==3.5.0 'uvicorn[standard]==0.30.3' && python -c \"
import asyncio, sys, threading, time, uvicorn
from fastapi import FastAPI

app = FastAPI()

@app.get('/health')
def health():
    return {'ok': True}

print(f'Before uvicorn: {type(asyncio.get_event_loop_policy()).__name__}')

def run_server():
    server = uvicorn.Server(uvicorn.Config(app=app, host='127.0.0.1', port=8080, log_level='error'))
    server.run()

threading.Thread(target=run_server, daemon=True).start()
time.sleep(1)

print(f'After uvicorn: {type(asyncio.get_event_loop_policy()).__name__}')

loop = asyncio.SelectorEventLoop()
asyncio.set_event_loop(loop)

async def test():
    proc = await asyncio.create_subprocess_exec(sys.executable, '-c', 'print(1)')
    await proc.wait()

loop.run_until_complete(test())
\"
"
```

## Fix

In `src/prefect/workers/server.py`, add `loop="asyncio"` to the uvicorn config to prevent uvloop from setting a global policy:

```python
config = uvicorn.Config(
    app=app,
    host=PREFECT_WORKER_WEBSERVER_HOST.value(),
    port=PREFECT_WORKER_WEBSERVER_PORT.value(),
    log_level=log_level,
    loop="asyncio",  # Prevent uvloop from setting global policy
)
```

## Workaround for users

Upgrade uvicorn: `uvicorn[standard]>=0.36.0`

## Version info

- Prefect: 3.5.0
- Python: 3.12.x / 3.13.x
- uvicorn: ≤0.35.0 with uvloop (via `uvicorn[standard]`)
prefecthq/prefect#19658.title: Workers with --with-healthcheck fail to spawn subprocesses when uvloop is installed

prefecthq/prefect#19682.body:
Implements the `/runs` page route in the React UI with row selection and bulk delete functionality for the Flow Runs tab.

## Changes

This PR wires together existing components to create a functional `/runs` page:

- **Route implementation**: Creates the `/runs` route with Flow Runs and Task Runs tabs
- **URL state management**: Adds zod-validated search params for pagination, sorting, search, and state filters
- **Row selection**: Integrates `FlowRunsRowCount` with select-all checkbox and `FlowRunsList` with individual row selection
- **Bulk delete**: Uses existing `useDeleteFlowRunsDialog` hook for confirmation modal and deletion
- **Data prefetching**: Loader prefetches flow runs count and paginated results for faster rendering
- **MSW mock**: Adds handler for `/flow_runs/paginate` endpoint to prevent test failures

## Implementation Notes

The implementation reuses existing components from `@/components/flow-runs/flow-runs-list/`:
- `FlowRunsFilters` - search, state filter, and sort controls
- `FlowRunsList` - list with optional row selection
- `FlowRunsRowCount` - count display with select-all and delete button
- `FlowRunsPagination` - pagination controls

The Task Runs tab currently shows a placeholder message.

## Human Review Checklist

- [ ] Verify row selection and bulk delete work correctly in the browser
- [ ] Confirm the Task Runs tab placeholder is acceptable for this scope
- [ ] Review the type cast at line 161 (`search.state as FlowRunState[]`) - relies on zod validation
- [ ] Consider if dedicated route tests should be added

---

**Link to Devin run**: https://app.devin.ai/sessions/5e9c39bd63ed487f92574a286ae24f1a
**Requested by**: alex.s@prefect.io (@desertaxle)

### Checklist

- [ ] This pull request references any related issue by including "closes `<link to issue>`"
  - If no issue exists and your change is not a small fix, please [create an issue](https://github.com/PrefectHQ/prefect/issues/new/choose) first.
- [ ] If this pull request adds new functionality, it includes unit tests that cover the changes
- [ ] If this pull request removes docs files, it includes redirect settings in `mint.json`.
- [ ] If this pull request adds functions or classes, it includes helpful docstrings.

prefecthq/prefect#19686.body:
### Bug summary

Versions
dbt 1.10.15
prefect-dbt 0.7.2

I've also tried this on dbt 1.8.0 with the same error, I couldn't go further back than that because of other issues that was causing.

We're trying to upgrade from DbtCoreOperation to PrefectDbtRunner, but we're running into an issue where PrefectDbtRunner is trying to add the flag `--target-path`. In the newer versions of dbt, this is not accepted and should just be `--target` so we're getting the error "No such option: --target-path" when trying to run it.

As far as I can see, there's no way to stop the class from adding in this flag.

prefecthq/prefect#19752.body:
A somewhat nicer interface would be to allow people to declare (or perhaps infer) when a `Variable` is declared its type - that way Prefect could offer type safety & narrowing directly.

e.g.

```python
var = Variable[str].get("my_variable")
```

_Originally posted by @sghegoiu-rokos in https://github.com/PrefectHQ/prefect/discussions/19746#discussioncomment-15241986_

prefecthq/prefect#19762.body:
### Bug summary

A `RuntimeWarning: coroutine 'wait' was never awaited` or `RuntimeWarning: coroutine 'sleep' was never awaited` warning is raised when the prefect test harness is used in an async context, as in this simple script:
```
import asyncio
from prefect.testing.utilities import prefect_test_harness

async def test_prefect_harness():
    with prefect_test_harness():
        pass

if __name__ == "__main__":
    asyncio.run(test_prefect_harness())
```

This is due to the recent addition of `EventsWorker.drain_all()` in the `prefect_test_harness` definition in #19343. The `drain_all` method returns async coroutines when run from an that should be awaited, but the test harness does not call it with `await`. This is causing improper cleanup, particularly in pytest with a test like 
```
async def async_test_with_prefect_harness():
    with prefect_test_harness(server_startup_timeout=60):
        client = get_client()
        async with ProcessWorker(work_pool_name="default-work-pool", limit=2) as worker:
            ...
```
which causes the following log:

prefecthq/prefect#19778.body:
### Bug summary

## Problem

When `concurrency_limit` is specified in `prefect.yaml` without `grace_period_seconds`, Prefect includes `grace_period_seconds: None` in the `concurrency_options` dict sent to the API, causing a 422 validation error.

## Expected Behavior

`grace_period_seconds` should be optional. If omitted from the YAML, it should either:
1. Not be included in the `concurrency_options` dict sent to the API, OR
2. Fall back to the server default (300 seconds) without validation errors

## Actual Behavior

Prefect includes `grace_period_seconds: None` in `concurrency_options`, which triggers API validation that requires an integer (60-86400).

## Error Message

```
prefect.exceptions.PrefectHTTPStatusError: Client error '422 Unprocessable Entity'
Response: {'exception_message': 'Invalid request received.', 'exception_detail': [{'type': 'int_type', 'loc': ['body', 'concurrency_options', 'grace_period_seconds'], 'msg': 'Input should be a valid integer', 'input': None}]}
```

## Minimal Reproduction

```
deployments:
  - name: "test_deployment"
    concurrency_limit:
      limit: 5
      collision_strategy: "ENQUEUE"
```

## Workaround

Explicitly setting `grace_period_seconds` to an integer value (e.g., `grace_period_seconds: 60`) works, but this shouldn't be required for an optional field.

## Related

- **Release**: [3.6.6 - The Eternal Docket of the Spotless Server](https://github.com/PrefectHQ/prefect/releases/tag/3.6.6) (released December 08, 2025) - This release added "client-side configuration for deployment concurrency grace period"
- **PR**: [#19489](https://github.com/PrefectHQ/prefect/pull/19489) - "Add client-side configuration for deployment concurrency grace period"
  - The PR code shows it should only be added when not None: `if grace_period_seconds is not None: concurrency_options["grace_period_seconds"] = grace_period_seconds`
  - However, the YAML processing appears to be adding `None` explicitly when the field is omitted

## Environment

- Prefect version: 3.6.6
- Deployment method: `prefect deploy` via `prefect.yaml`

## Workaround
Explicitly setting `grace_period_seconds` to an integer value (e.g., `grace_period_seconds: 60`) works, but this shouldn't be required for an optional field.

## Related
- Release: [3.6.6](https://github.com/PrefectHQ/prefect/releases/tag/3.6.6) (released December 08, 2025) - This release added "client-side configuration for deployment concurrency grace period"
- PR: #19489 - "Add client-side configuration for deployment concurrency grace period"
    - The PR code shows it should only be added when not None: `if grace_period_seconds is not None: concurrency_options["grace_period_seconds"] = grace_period_seconds`
    - However, the YAML processing appears to be adding None explicitly when the field is omitted

```Text
Server type:          cloud
```

prefecthq/prefect#19831.body:
### Describe the current behavior

The [`AWSCredentials`](https://docs.prefect.io/integrations/prefect-aws/index#credentials) block does not support AWS IAM Assume Role functionality.

Having AssumeRole support would be a big win because it matches AWS best practices: short-lived, automatically rotated credentials; least privilege via role scoping; and clean separation between “who I am” and “what I can do” across accounts and environments. In fact, this is the second recommendation under [AWS's security best practices for IAM](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/IAM/latest/UserGuide/best-practices.html#bp-workloads-use-roles).

It would let Prefect users model the full auth chain declaratively in one place (the block), making deployments more reproducible, reducing secret sprawl, and enabling common enterprise setups (cross-account S3, Secrets Manager, ECS, etc.) without bespoke glue code or environment-specific hacks.

### Describe the proposed behavior

Add two new optional fields to the current `AWSCredentials` Block:

- `role_arn` - the role to assume
- `assume_role_kwargs` - these are passed to the Boto3 STS Client [`assume_role` method](https://boto3.amazonaws.com/v1/documentation/api/latest/reference/services/sts/client/assume_role.html) call. For example, many AWS IAM roles have an `ExternalID` defined to prevent the [confused deputy problem](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/IAM/latest/UserGuide/confused-deputy.html).

If `role_arn` is set, take the base set of credentials defined and apply them to the `assume_role` invocation. Return the resulting temporary credentials.

Current behavior does not change and the block is fully backwards compatible.

### Example Use

Use an instance role's credentials to assume another role:

```python
aws_credentials = AwsCredentials(
    role_arn="arn:aws:iam::123456789012:role/MyRole"
)
s3_client = aws_credentials.get_boto3_session().client("s3")
```

Customize the inputs to the `assume_role` call:

```python
aws_credentials = AwsCredentials(
    role_arn="arn:aws:iam::123456789012:role/MyRole",
    assume_role_kwargs={
        "RoleSessionName": "my-session",
        "DurationSeconds": 3600,
        "ExternalId": "unique-external-id"
    }
)
s3_client = aws_credentials.get_boto3_session().client("s3")
```

### Additional context

_No response_
prefecthq/prefect#19831.title: Support for AWS IAM Assume Role authentication in AWSCredentials Block

prefecthq/prefect#19861.body:
### Bug summary

### Problem
When using a block reference in a deployment YAML for git clone credentials:
```yaml
pull:
  - prefect.deployments.steps.git_clone:
      repository: https://gitlab.com/org/project/repo.git
      credentials: "{{ prefect.blocks.gitlab-credentials.my-block }}"
```

The credentials appear as a `dict` in `src/prefect/runner/storage.py` instead of a `Block` instance.

### Root Cause
`resolve_block_document_references` in `src/prefect/utilities/templating.py` returns block data (dict) instead of Block instances when resolving full block references.

### Impact
Blocks implementing `_GitCredentialsFormatter` (e.g., `GitLabCredentials`, `GitHubCredentials`) fail because `GitRepository` checks `isinstance(self._credentials, _GitCredentialsFormatter)`, which fails when credentials is a dict.

### Potential areas for resolution
1. Modify `resolve_block_document_references` in `src/prefect/utilities/templating.py` to return Block instances when resolving full block references (no keypath).
2. Update `apply_values` in `src/prefect/utilities/templating.py` to return non-template objects (e.g., Block instances) unchanged instead of raising `ValueError`.

This ensures credentials blocks are passed as Block instances to `GitRepository`, enabling `format_git_credentials()` to work correctly.


### Version info

```Text
Version:              3.6.2
API version:          0.8.4
Python version:       3.12.8
Git commit:           6e305792
Built:                Fri, Nov 14, 2025 12:15 AM
OS/Arch:              darwin/arm64
Profile:              local
Server type:          server
Pydantic version:     2.12.4
Server:
  Database:           sqlite
  SQLite version:     3.47.1
```

### Additional context

This issue can be seen in #19296 after #19236 was implemented.

The code partially works because users are able to manually update the username/token.

prefecthq/prefect#19903.body:
## Overview

This is a **long-lived tracking issue** for making the Prefect CLI more friendly for automation, scripting, and AI agent usage. As LLMs and agents increasingly interact with CLIs, and as users build more sophisticated automation pipelines, the CLI needs to support non-interactive, batch, and machine-readable workflows.

## Pain Points

When using the CLI programmatically (scripts, CI/CD, agents), several friction points emerge:

1. **No batch operations** - Commands like `flow-run delete` only accept a single ID, requiring loops for bulk operations
2. **Inconsistent confirmation prompts** - Some commands prompt for confirmation, others don't; no universal `--yes`/`-y` flag
3. **No query-based operations** - Can't delete/cancel based on filters (e.g., "all pending runs with no deployment")
4. **Limited machine-readable output** - Addressed partially by #19483 and #6930, but still gaps

## Proposed Improvements

### Batch Operations
- Accept multiple IDs: `prefect flow-run delete <id1> <id2> <id3>`
- Accept IDs from stdin: `echo "id1\nid2" | prefect flow-run delete --stdin`

### Query-Based Operations  
- Delete/cancel by filter: `prefect flow-run delete --state PENDING --deployment-id null`
- Dry-run mode: `prefect flow-run delete --state FAILED --dry-run` (show what would be deleted)

### Consistent Confirmation Flags
- Universal `--yes`/`-y` flag for all destructive operations (some commands have it, some don't)

### Agent-Friendly Error Messages
- Structured error output (JSON errors with `--output json`)
- Clear exit codes for different failure modes
- Actionable error messages

## Related Issues

- #19483 - Add `-o json` output option to CLI ls commands
- #6930 - Add batch mode / plain output feature to CLI

---

This issue will remain open as a tracking issue. Individual improvements should be opened as separate issues referencing this one.
prefecthq/prefect#19903.title: CLI improvements for automation and agent usage

prefecthq/prefect#19935.body:
### Bug summary

I recently spent way too much time debugging what looked like random 500 errors coming from the Prefect server.

The failures were happening in a pretty consistent pattern: deployment calls and some task calls that accepted a Pydantic model as an argument. Since Prefect is an optional dependency in our codebase, we sometimes strip block_type_slug out of the JSON so it’ll validate against our Pydantic models. This has worked fine for a while, but a recent change caused that modified object to get passed back into Prefect—both during scheduling and inside a task call.

What we eventually figured out is that if you:

1. Read a block document from Prefect
2. Remove block_type_slug from it
3. Pass that object back to Prefect as a parameter

prefecthq/prefect#19937.body:
### Describe the current behavior

k8s worker observes job and pod events by default. On each start it received **all** events. For each crashed job it checks if corresponding flow run is in "crashed" started, for each pod event it checks if such an event has been already recorded. Important here is that observer receives all event on every start, it is completely stateless. With tens of thousands of pod events it overloads both worker and server, observer cpu is 100% for tens of minutes, server stops responding api requests.
While this is bullet-proof design, I'd rather sacrifice same pod events and ignore all events that happened before worker start, or N minutes before worker start. This can be implemented as easy as 
```
    event_type = event["type"]
    phase = status["phase"]
    if event_type is None:
        logger.info("Skipping pod event, probably an old one")
        return
```
Significantly reduces load on startup. As worker can (and probably should) be run with multiple replicas, no events would be lost, unless deployment is scaled down to zero.

### Describe the proposed behavior

Disable processing past pod and jobs events. Probably make it configurable:
- either enabled as it is now
- disabled with optional "expiration" period, `lastTransitionTime` of an event should not be older than N minutes.

Does that make sense? 

### Example Use

_No response_

### Additional context

_No response_
prefecthq/prefect#19937.title: Ignore past k8s events in worker's observer

prefecthq/prefect#19949.body:
### Bug summary

Trying to send string data via a CustomWebhook block, however the form_data field (mapping to httpx data=...) only accepts `dict[str, str]`.
E.g. trying to setup a payload such as `httpx.post(..., data="test")` is valid, however not possible when using the CustomWebhook.

Is it expected for the `form_data` to be of type object / open for change to support both object & string / just string?
We were able to validate what we are trying to do by over-writing the block, however that seems like a workaround.

```python
from typing import Optional

from pydantic import Field
from prefect.blocks.notifications import CustomWebhookNotificationBlock


class DatadogEventWebhook(CustomWebhookNotificationBlock):
    _block_type_name = "DatadogEventWebhook"

    form_data: Optional[str] = Field(
        title="Form Data",
        description=(
            "Send form data as payload. Should not be used together with _JSON Data_."
        ),
        examples=[
            'Custom form data'
        ],
    )


DatadogEventWebhook.register_type_and_schema()
```

This relates to discussion https://github.com/PrefectHQ/prefect/discussions/17946, we are trying to send an event (in this case to datadog) for every flow state change, using an automation. The event being sent will need to transfer information from the automation (e.g. flow name, etc) to the webhook payload being sent.

Being able to directly sent json from the automation `send-notification` action does not seem to be possible, hence us trying to send a string as form data, manually setting the content-type to `application/json`.

### Version info

```Text
We use 3.4.18 on the server side with python 3.12, however also validated this is still not possible with the latest 3.6.7

Below output of running locally, which was used to validate the block over-writing behaviour

Version:              3.4.18
API version:          0.8.4
Python version:       3.12.12
Git commit:           3baaf4f4
Built:                Fri, Sep 12, 2025 04:03 PM
OS/Arch:              win32/AMD64
Profile:              local
Server type:          server
Pydantic version:     2.12.3
Server:
  Database:           sqlite
  SQLite version:     3.50.4
```
